content.problemStatement:
You are given two sorted arrays, arr1arr1arr1 and arr2arr2arr2. Your task is to find the union of these two arrays. The union includes all distinct elements from both arrays in ascending order.

Write a function that returns the union of arr1arr1arr1 and arr2arr2arr2 in a single sorted array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input:

The first line contains two integers NNN and MMM, denoting the size of the two arrays.
The next two lines denotes the two arrays arr1arr1arr1 and arr2arr2arr2 of size NNN and MMM respectively.




Output Format
For each test case, output a sorted array containing the distinct elements from both arr1arr1arr1 and arr2arr2arr2.
constraints: Both input arrays are sorted in non‑decreasing order
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of three lines: the first line contains two integers N and M, the sizes of the two arrays. The second line contains N space‑separated integers – the elements of the first sorted array. The third line contains M space‑separated integers – the elements of the second sorted array. All arrays are sorted in non‑decreasing order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the union of the two arrays – all distinct elements that appear in either array – sorted in ascending order and separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1 merges the two sorted arrays and removes duplicates, yielding 1 2 3 4 5 7 10. Test case 2 has all distinct elements, so the union is the sorted concatenation.
All elements are distinct; the union is simply the sorted combination of both arrays.
Both arrays contain only the value 1, so the union consists of a single element.

solution.approach: Because both input arrays are already sorted, we can obtain their union in linear time using the two‑pointer technique. Maintain two indices i and j for the two arrays and a variable last to remember the last printed value. At each step compare arr1[i] and arr2[j]: the smaller value (if different from last) is appended to the answer and its pointer is advanced; if they are equal, append it once and advance both pointers. After one array is exhausted, flush the remaining elements of the other array, again skipping duplicates. This runs in O(N+M) per test case and uses O(1) extra space besides the output list.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N + M) per test case, O(∑(N+M)) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (output list not counted)
